summary: High‑energy half‑step opener, soaring Franklin’s Tower & crisp drums
blurb: The show kicks off with a blistering Mississippi Half‑Step > Franklin's Tower that sets an upbeat tone. Highlights include Peggy‑O, Looks Like Rain and an electrifying Eyes Of The World, capped by a tight Drums > Not Fade Away transition. Overall the band is locked in, delivering a solid first set and a high‑energy second.
review: December 12, 1978 at Miami’s Jai‑Alai Fronton finds the Dead riding a wave of momentum from start to finish. A scorching Mississippi Half‑Step Uptown Toodeloo segues straight into Franklin's Tower, launching a set that never loses its spark. The first half rolls through Looks Like Rain and a beautifully rendered Peggy‑O before cruising through classic rockers like Big River and Ramble On Rose. The second set turns up the dial: Bertha and Good Lovin' flow into Brown Eyed Women, then an inspired Estimated Prophet leads into a soaring Eyes Of The World that blossoms into a thunderous Drums > Not Fade Away jam and a raucous Goin’ Down The Road Feeling Bad/Around & Around finish. Jerry’s guitar is on fire—elegant licks, precise soloing—and Keith’s electric‑piano adds color throughout, while Mickey and Bill lock together for a hand‑drummed groove that propels the jams. Fans will especially love the high‑energy Half‑Step > Franklin's sequence and the crisp, tight drum‑to‑fade‑away transition, making this Miami gig a must‑listen snapshot of late ’78 firepower.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: All four recordings are marked positive, repeatedly praising the high‑energy Half‑Step > Franklin's suite, soaring Eyes Of The World, tight Drums > Not Fade Away and overall band cohesion. No negative comments appear across analyses, just consistent praise for Jerry’s lead work, Keith’s keyboards and the dual‑drum interplay.

best_recording.reason: Clear soundboard with good mix captures the soaring Help/Slip/Franklin suite and Peggy‑O, offering the most listenable and detailed representation of the show.

key_highlights: Soaring Mississippi Half‑Step > Franklin's Tower opener sets an upbeat tone
High‑energy Eyes Of The World jam with tight dual‑drum work
Crisp Drums > Not Fade Away transition that drives the second set
Beautifully rendered Peggy‑O and vibrant Looks Like Rain

band_performance.Jerry: Garcia delivers strong, elegant playing with fiery lead work, precise soloing and full‑throated vocals throughout.
band_performance.Phil: Phil provides a steaming bass foundation that pushes the band forward, especially evident in the high‑energy second set.
band_performance.Bob: Weir is having fun on stage, adding vocal flair and shout‑outs (e.g., Good Lovin') that lift the vibe.
band_performance.Keith: Keith cooks up electric‑piano textures—"bangin'" and colorful—though he sounds a touch off later in the set.
band_performance.Mickey: Mickey’s drumming is clear, syncopated and adds a pleasing slap‑back rhythm; his interplay with Bill shines during Drums > Not Fade Away.
band_performance.Billy: Bill delivers tight, high‑energy playing, especially in the second‑set jams and the drum‑to‑fade‑away segment.

must_listen_sequences: Goin' Down The Road Feeling Bad
